mnemonics: D.O.T.S. for Inner Products: D - Dot product is the archetype, O - Orthogonality defines perpendicularity, T - Triangle and Cauchy-Schwarz inequalities, S - Symmetry and positive definiteness are key axioms
keyPoints: An inner product is a generalization of the dot product to abstract vector spaces
The axioms of an inner product are conjugate symmetry, linearity in the first argument, and positive-definiteness
Inner products induce norms, which allow for the definition of length and distance in vector spaces
commonMistakes: Forgetting that the inner product is linear only in the first argument for complex spaces (conjugate linear in the second)
Confusing the inner product with a norm (the norm is derived from the inner product via ||v|| = √⟨v,v⟩)
Assuming all vector spaces have a natural inner product (must be explicitly defined)
